title: Vellayani Devi Temple
tagline: Vellayani Devi Temple — spiritual town of Kerala

overview.short: The Major Vellayani Devi Temple is a highly revered shrine in Kerala, India, dedicated to goddess Bhadrakali.
overview.description: The Major Vellayani Devi Temple is a highly revered shrine in Kerala, India, dedicated to goddess Bhadrakali. The shrine is situated in Vellayani 1.5 km west of Vellayani junction, 12 km southeast of Thiruvananthapuram on the eastern bank of Vellayani Lake. The temple is under the management of Travancore Devaswom Board. The temple structure has a bronze roof with traditional art work and carries Dravidian architecture.

topPlaces.name: Vellayani
topPlaces.description: Vellayani is a suburb of Thiruvananthapuram, the capital city of Kerala, India.

topPlaces.name: Kanthari international
topPlaces.description: kanthari is an educational and training institute at Thiruvananthapuram, Kerala, India. It was co-founded by Sabriye Tenberken and Paul Kronenberg in 2005, with the first members jointing in 2009, as an extension of their pioneering project Braille without borders.

topPlaces.name: Vellayani Lake
topPlaces.description: Vellayani Lake, or Vellayani Kayal, is the second largest freshwater lake in the state of Kerala and is situated in Thiruvananthapuram, Kerala, India. Vellayani is known for its lotus blooms and is considered a little eco-haven.

topPlaces.name: Nemom
topPlaces.description: Nemom is a suburb of Thiruvananthapuram, the capital city of Kerala, India. It is surrounded by Thirumala in the north, Malayinkeezhu in the north-east, Balaramapuram in the south-east, Kovalam in the south and Thiruvallam in the west.

topPlaces.name: Choozhattukotta
topPlaces.description: Choozhattukotta is a village situated on the perimeter of Thiruvananthapuram, Kerala, India. It is located 9 km from Trivandrum Central.

topPlaces.name: Arikkadamukku
topPlaces.description: Arikkadamukku is a place in Thiruvananthapuram district, Kerala, India. It is located 9 kilometres (5.6 mi) south of Thiruvananthapuram city centre and is a part of Pallichal village.

topPlaces.name: Keleswaram
topPlaces.description: Keleswaram is a small village in Thiruvananthapuram district in the state of Kerala, India. This place is located between Peringammala and Punnamoodu .

topPlaces.name: Pappanamcode
topPlaces.description: Pappanamcode(Abode of Mahouts) is a locality of Thiruvananthapuram, the capital of Kerala, India. Only 5 km from Trivandrum central railway station and bus stand.

items.note: Vellayani is a suburb of Thiruvananthapuram, the capital city of Kerala, India.

kanthari is an educational and training institute at Thiruvananthapuram, Kerala, India. It was co-founded by Sabriye Tenberken and Paul Kronenberg in 2005, with the first members jointing in 2009, as an extension of their pioneering project Braille without borders.

Vellayani Lake, or Vellayani Kayal, is the second largest freshwater lake in the state of Kerala and is situated in Thiruvananthapuram, Kerala, India. Vellayani is known for its lotus blooms and is considered a little eco-haven.

Nemom is a suburb of Thiruvananthapuram, the capital city of Kerala, India. It is surrounded by Thirumala in the north, Malayinkeezhu in the north-east, Balaramapuram in the south-east, Kovalam in the south and Thiruvallam in the west.
Choozhattukotta is a village situated on the perimeter of Thiruvananthapuram, Kerala, India. It is located 9 km from Trivandrum Central.
Arikkadamukku is a place in Thiruvananthapuram district, Kerala, India. It is located 9 kilometres (5.6 mi) south of Thiruvananthapuram city centre and is a part of Pallichal village.
Keleswaram is a small village in Thiruvananthapuram district in the state of Kerala, India. This place is located between Peringammala and Punnamoodu .
Pappanamcode(Abode of Mahouts) is a locality of Thiruvananthapuram, the capital of Kerala, India. Only 5 km from Trivandrum central railway station and bus stand.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Vellayani Devi Temple?
faq.a: The best time to visit Vellayani Devi Temple is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Vellayani Devi Temple?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Thiruvananthapuram International Airport (9 km) and the nearest railway station is Thiruvananthapuram Central (6 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Vellayani Devi Temple?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Vellayani Devi Temple at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Vellayani Devi Temple?
faq.a: Stays in Vellayani Devi Temple start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Vellayani Devi Temple?
faq.a: The top attractions are Vellayani, Kanthari international, Vellayani Lake, Nemom, Choozhattukotta.
